name: Wild-type FMT improved progeroid mouse healthspan and lifespan
summary: Wild-type donor FMT provides mammalian lifespan and healthspan support in accelerated-aging models.
statement: In two progeroid mouse models, fecal microbiota transplantation from wild-type mice enhanced healthspan and lifespan, while Akkermansia muciniphila was sufficient to produce beneficial effects and metabolomics implicated secondary bile-acid restoration.
population_or_model: Two progeroid mouse models with human progeria and centenarian microbiome context
time_horizon: Healthspan and lifespan follow-up in accelerated-aging models
quantitative_note: The PubMed abstract reports enhanced healthspan and lifespan but does not provide numeric effect sizes.
caveats: The intervention was tested in progeroid models rather than naturally aged animals.
Akkermansia sufficiency overlaps live-biotherapeutic and microbiome-composition tracks.
Human progeria dysbiosis context is observational and does not establish human FMT efficacy.
